commit.message:
Add comprehensive WASM feature parity tests

- Created cmd/wasm/feature_parity_test.go with 5 comprehensive test functions
- TestWASMFeatureParity validates all 9 core analyzers (functions, structs,
  interfaces, packages, patterns, concurrency, duplication, naming, documentation)
  produce identical results between CLI and WASM builds
- TestWASMJSONOutputFormat validates JSON serialization in WASM context
- TestWASMEmptyInput verifies graceful empty directory handling
- TestWASMInvalidInput verifies error handling for invalid Go code
- TestWASMConfigurationParity validates config options (SkipTestFiles, complexity thresholds)
- All tests pass, confirming README claim of CLI/WASM feature parity is accurate
- Marked FUNCTIONAL_AUDIT.md task as resolved

This addresses the audit finding: 'WebAssembly feature parity claim unverified'
by providing automated differential testing between CLI and WASM analysis workflows.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: shadepants <96809510+shadepants@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: defer animosity mutation past storyteller suppression check

Move rel.animosity increment for military_buildup out of
deriveConsequence and into phaseCascade, gated on the
shouldSuppressEvent check. Previously the world-state mutation fired
unconditionally even when the event was budget-suppressed, causing ghost
animosity accumulation on suppressed cascade events.

Also remove committed .claude/settings.local.json and add .claude/ to
.gitignore so tool config stays local.

commit.message:
Refactor footer to use StoreWidget self-subscription (#83)

* Migrate StatusFooter to StoreWidget and remove push_footer reaction

- Introduce StoreWidget mixin so widgets self-subscribe to the view-store
  instead of being push-updated by view_store_bridge callbacks
- Migrate StatusFooter to StoreWidget; remove push_footer reaction,
  enrich_footer_state helper, and all manual update_display call sites
- Remove legacy app resume state translation (_apply_resume_legacy_app_state,
  _legacy_resume_panel_updates); panel:* keys round-trip cleanly now
- Add _SE/_FE VisState shorthands; update DEFAULT_FILTERSETS to use expanded
  variants where appropriate; default filter:active to "1"
- Add hover_label support to Chip with on_enter/on_leave handlers
- Register store_widget in hot-reload _RELOAD_ORDER

* cleanup: remove obsolete search edit no-op and stale baseline entries

commit.message:
fix(workflow): restore ac-tdd-sprint as separate phase with ac-codex agent

YAML had a missing '- id: ac-tdd-sprint' separator causing TDD sprint
content to be merged into ac-inception (overriding its pattern/name).
Result: inception was running as loop with 'worker' agent, no TDD sprint.

Now 6 correct phases:
- ac-inception: sequential, ac-architect, gate=all_approved
- ac-tdd-sprint: loop, ac-codex, gate=all_approved  <- restored
- ac-adversarial-check: sequential, ac-adversarial, gate=no_veto
- ac-qa-sprint: parallel, [ac-qa-agent, ft-e2e-ihm], gate=all_approved
- ac-cicd: sequential, ac-cicd-agent, gate=no_veto
- ac-coach-review: sequential, ac-coach, gate=always

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

test: add Jest unit + Vitest A/B integration tests for git history search

- Jest (unit): github-ingest-history.test.ts
  - Tests commit formatting, pagination via Link header, error resilience
  - Fully mocked — no PGlite, no network

- Vitest (integration): github-history-search.vitest.ts
  - Real PGlite in-memory with molecules table + GIN FTS index
  - Condition A: code-only molecule — commit-specific terms return 0 results
  - Condition B: commit history molecule ingested — same terms return results
  - Also verifies author name, sha prefix, stop-word resilience, bucket isolation

- vitest.config.ts: pool=forks (PGlite WASM global state), 60s timeout
  - Removed orphaned @jest/globals PGlite tests (would throw under Vitest)
  - Fixed deprecated poolOptions -> top-level forks option (Vitest 4)

- engine/package.json: added test:vitest + test:vitest:watch scripts, vitest devDep

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.description: Bruno CLI and exposes an observable execution lifecycle for external integrations. It runs Bruno collections through bru run, captures process output, parses generated reports, and emits typed lifecycle events such as run start/end, request execution, test results, assertions, stdout, stderr. Adapter is designed to be independent from Bruno’s core

[SPEC-06] fix: profile management UI — fix Create button, remove AppWindow button, fix window watcher auto-switch, add Clone From on profile creation (#3)

* Initial plan

* feat(spec-06): Preferences & Profile Configuration

- Add preferences table (key/value store) to schema.sql
- Create preferences.repo.ts with CRUD operations
- Create preferences API routes (GET/PUT /api/preferences)
- Create SSE events endpoint (GET /api/events)
- Add event emitter to memory-store for real-time push
- Add appliedProfileId to frontend context (distinguishes viewed vs active profile)
- Add "● Active" badge in profile selector dropdown
- Fix window watcher fallback: active → default → first (not by name)
- Persist window watcher toggle in preferences
- Restore watcher state and last active profile on bootstrap
- Add SSE subscription in AppContext for real-time updates
- Add active-profile API endpoints (GET/POST /api/active-profile)
- Update BootstrapData types to include preferences and activeProfileId
- Add 9 tests for preferences.repo
- All 163 tests pass

Co-authored-by: gabrielspisso <11954510+gabrielspisso@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address code review feedback for spec-06

- Add ProfileSwitchedPayload type for consistent event payloads
- Add error handling to SSE endpoint with try-catch
- Handle connection close errors gracefully in SSE write

Co-authored-by: gabrielspisso <11954510+gabrielspisso@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add profile management UI (create, delete, app matching)

- Add "+" button in Topbar to create new blank profiles with optional window classes
- Add app matching button (window icon) to set window classes per profile
- Add delete button to remove current profile
- Profile dropdown now shows [window classes] next to profile names
- Add createNewProfile, deleteCurrentProfile, updateProfileMeta to AppContext
- Add updateProfile API function in useApi.ts
- New Profile modal with name + window classes input
- Profile Settings modal to edit window classes for existing profiles
- CSS for modals and new topbar buttons

Co-authored-by: gabrielspisso <11954510+gabrielspisso@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address code review feedback

- Fix empty window classes sent as [] instead of undefined when clearing
- Simplify ternary for windowClasses check in createNewProfile
- Improve readability of profile dropdown option template

Co-authored-by: gabrielspisso <11954510+gabrielspisso@users.noreply.github.com>

* Changes before error encountered

Co-authored-by: gabrielspisso <11954510+gabrielspisso@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: render profile modal via React Portal to fix Create button always disabled

The modal was clipped by stacking context from topbar's backdrop-filter +
position:sticky + parent overflow:hidden, hiding the Profile Name input.
createPortal(modal, document.body) bypasses all ancestor stacking contexts.

Co-authored-by: gabrielspisso <11954510+gabrielspisso@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: window watcher auto-switches profiles based on windowClasses

Server: unified match/fallback logic — matches windowClasses first,
falls back to Default profile. Tracks lastWatcherProfileId to skip
redundant switches. Updates activeProfileId + persists preference.

Frontend: SSE handler now switches the full UI (activeProfileId,
buttons, dirty/save/apply state) on watcher trigger, shows profile
name in toast.

Co-authored-by: gabrielspisso <11954510+gabrielspisso@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: watcher polls every 2s with composite-key dedup and case-insensitive matching

windowWatcher.ts: remove the "only emit on change" guard so the event
fires on every poll. This picks up newly-created profiles even when the
active window hasn't changed (e.g. user creates a firefox profile while
already in Firefox).

server/index.ts: replace lastWatcherProfileId (profile ID only) with a
composite key "windowClass::profileId" so dedup skips only when both the
active window AND the target profile are unchanged. Adds case-insensitive
windowClasses matching (wc.toLowerCase()) so "Firefox" == "firefox"
regardless of capitalisation.

Co-authored-by: gabrielspisso <11954510+gabrielspisso@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: revert watcher to emit-on-change, commit dedup key before apply

windowWatcher.ts: revert to only emitting on window class change.
Emitting on every poll caused Solaar to be applied every 2 s, creating
an infinite restart loop.

server/index.ts: move lastWatcherKey = key to BEFORE applyProfileToSolaar.
Previously the key was only set on success, so any slow/failing apply
would hammer Solaar on every subsequent poll. Setting the key eagerly
commits to the (window, profile) decision once — Solaar is only called
once per window change regardless of apply outcome.

Co-authored-by: gabrielspisso <11954510+gabrielspisso@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add "Clone from" selector to New Profile modal

Topbar.tsx: add <select> "Clone from" with None + all existing profiles.
AppContext.tsx: createNewProfile accepts optional cloneFromProfileId —
deep-clones (JSON round-trip) buttons from the source profile, falls back
to blank defaults when None is selected or source not found.

Co-authored-by: gabrielspisso <11954510+gabrielspisso@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: gabrielspisso <11954510+gabrielspisso@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Promote skill spec conformance checks from warnings to errors (#278)

* Promote skill spec conformance checks from warnings to errors

The agentskills.io spec (https://agentskills.io/specification) uses
'Must' for all of these constraints:

- name: 'Must be 1-64 characters'
- name: 'May only contain lowercase alphanumeric and hyphens'
- name: 'Must not start or end with -'
- name: 'Must not contain consecutive hyphens'
- name: 'Must match the parent directory name'
- description: 'Must be 1-1024 characters' (non-empty)
- compatibility: 'Must be 1-500 characters if provided'

These were previously warnings following the lenient-loading guidance
from the client implementation guide, but that guidance is for client
loaders, not publishing validators. For a CI tool that gates what gets
published, spec violations should be blocking errors.

All 25 existing skills already comply — no changes needed to skills.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address PR review feedback: empty compatibility, empty name, test

- Error when compatibility field is provided but empty/whitespace,
  matching spec requirement of 1-500 characters when provided.
- Add explicit check for empty/whitespace name with targeted error
  message instead of falling through to regex mismatch.
- Add test for empty compatibility string rejection.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

strings interpolated into JS double-quoted contexts:
- robots.rs: fetch() URL now escaped via serde_json::to_string
- agent.rs: WCAG audit level parameter now escaped via json_escape

json_escape() output was placed, since json_escape already returns quoted
strings. Fixed in ws_connect, ws_intercept, ws_send, ws_messages, ws_close,
sse_listen, sse_messages, graphql_subscribe, and human_profile handlers.

commit.message:
feat(messaging): implement EditMessage RPC + edits_message_id delivery

construct-broker/types.rs:
- Add edits_message_id: Option<String> field to KafkaMessageEnvelope
  (serde skip_if_none for backward compat with existing Redis entries)
- Add KafkaMessageEnvelope::from_edit() constructor
  — sets edits_message_id so recipient client can apply in-place replacement

construct-queue/lib.rs:
- Add MessageQueue::increment_edit_count(message_id, ttl_secs) -> Result<u32>
  Uses Redis INCR + EXPIRE (set on first increment only)
  Key: edits:{message_id}, TTL: configurable (default 7 days)

proto/services/messaging_service.proto:
- Add string recipient_user_id = 11 to EditMessageRequest
  Server cannot route without it (no message DB)

messaging-service/main.rs:
  Auth → validate fields → Redis INCR (cap 20 edits) → from_edit envelope
  → write_message_to_user_stream → EditMessageResponse{success, edited_at, edit_count}
- convert_kafka_envelope_to_proto: propagate envelope.edits_message_id to proto

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
ci: add Jenkins declarative pipeline (Jenkinsfile)

Five-stage BUILD quality gate:
  1. Checkout         - pull source from SCM
  2. Install          - pip install -r requirements.txt
  3. Lint             - flake8 app.py tests/ --max-line-length=120
  4. Test             - pytest tests/ -v
  5. Docker Build     - docker build -t aceest-fitness:\ .

Post actions: success/failure echo messages for build status.
Image tagged with Jenkins BUILD_NUMBER for traceability.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

ci: add GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline

.github/workflows/main.yml — three-job pipeline:
  Job 1 (build-and-lint): installs Python deps + flake8 lint check
  Job 2 (docker-build):   builds Docker image, verifies creation
  Job 3 (test):           builds image + runs pytest INSIDE container
    docker run --rm -e DB_NAME=test_aceest.db aceest-fitness:test
                   python -m pytest tests/ -v --tb=short

Tests run inside the containerised environment per assignment spec.
Jobs 2 and 3 both depend on Job 1; run in parallel on success.
Triggers: push/PR to main or develop.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

chore: add Dockerfile and .dockerignore for containerisation

Dockerfile:
  - Base: python:3.11-slim (minimal attack surface)
  - Non-root user appuser for security
  - Layer-cached pip install (requirements before app code)
  - DB_NAME env var for runtime/test DB override
  - tests/ included so pytest can run inside container
  - CMD overridable: docker run ... python -m pytest tests/

.dockerignore excludes venv/, *.db, .git/, .pytest_cache/ from
build context to keep image lean.

commit.message:
[WIP] Fix package conflict error when updating app from debug APK (#53)

* Initial plan

* Fix package conflict: use consistent debug keystore for CI builds

Debug APKs built by GitHub Actions were signed with a freshly-generated
debug keystore on each runner VM, meaning two CI runs could produce APKs
with different certificates. Android rejects updates when certificates
don't match (INSTALL_FAILED_UPDATE_INCOMPATIBLE / "package conflict").

Fix:
- Add keystore/debug.keystore (standard Android debug credentials,
  intentionally public — never used for release)
- Configure the debug buildType in app/build.gradle to use this keystore
  so every CI build is signed with the same certificate
- Allow keystore/debug.keystore in .gitignore (exception to *.keystore)

Co-authored-by: hyliankid14 <1757523+hyliankid14@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: hyliankid14 <1757523+hyliankid14@users.noreply.github.com>

  derives the username from WebConfig.test_email (FIREBASE_TEST_EMAIL
  prefix before '@'), defaulting to 'ci-test@mytube.test'
- Update all 5 test methods to accept test_username as a fixture parameter
- Add testing/tests/MYTUBE-293/README.md documenting the scenario,
  preconditions, environment variables, and how to run locally

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

jtd.setTheme('octo') loads just-the-docs-octo.css, which didn't exist.
The theme only generates default/light/dark CSS files automatically.

commit.message:
Merge pull request #1 from dchu3/feat/gemini-cli-agent

feat: add Gemini-powered CLI agent for solana-x402-mcp

- Interactive readline CLI with natural language interface
- Gemini agentic tool-calling loop (auto-discovers and invokes MCP tools)
- MCP client over stdio to spawn and connect to solana-x402-mcp
- Environment-based configuration with .env support
- Supports all MCP server tools: wallet balance, send USDC, incoming payments, x402

commit.message:
Add News model with ActionText rich content (#212)

* Add News model with ActionText rich content (#185)

Install ActionText for rich text support. Create News model with title,
status (draft/published), optional game link, author, and published_at.
Add associations on User and Game models. Include factory and full spec
coverage with 100% mutant kill rate.
